title: When Playdate Stopped Being Fun
summary: For almost six years, Playdate (a little handheld gaming device with a crank input and 1-bit screen) was a space where I did some of my most creative and personal work. I embraced its quirks, shipped over twenty releases, and explored what a tiny, constrained device could do. It brought me joy, challenge, and a sense of purpose. But over time, that feeling…
content:
For almost six years, Playdate (a little handheld gaming device with a crank input and 1-bit screen) was a space where I did some of my most creative and personal work. I embraced its quirks, shipped over twenty releases, and explored what a tiny, constrained device could do. It brought me joy, challenge, and a sense of purpose.

But over time, that feeling changed. Following a difficult incident in October 2024 involving targeted harassment—admitted to by the developer responsible, but met with no meaningful follow-up from the community moderators and no reply at the time of writing from Panic—I began to feel increasingly disconnected from the scene. I gave it time, hoped things might shift, but ultimately the silence made it hard to keep building with the same heart.

So, I’m stepping back. Not dramatically—just with clarity. Right now, this isn’t the environment I can thrive in. If that changes, I’d be happy to return. For now, I want to leave a record of what I made. I’m proud of all of it. I’ll continue as a full-time indie developer, but in another castle.

Be excellent to each other. And have fun.

Released Projects

  • 2022-03 Today (the first Playdate thing sold on itch.io)
  • 2022-04 Horse Race
  • 2022-04 Audition
  • 2022-04 Prototypes multi-pack
  • 2022-04 Daily Driver (tech demo)
  • 2022-05 Circular
  • 2022-05 ST-DIN and Supermini fonts
  • 2023-04 Sparrow Solitaire (with Mac Vogelsang)
  • 2023-05 Piskel for Playdate (desktop pixel art app)
  • 2023-07 Mandala
  • 2023-08 Strategies
  • 2023-08 DateDiff
  • 2023-08 YOYOZO (“Best Games of 2023”, Ars Technica)
  • 2023-10 Kye (with Jan Martinek)
  • 2023-10 Super ICARUS
  • 2023-12 Fore! Track
  • 2023-12 See the Sky (with Thoru Yamamoto)
  • 2024-05 Rorschach (featuring mouse control)
  • 2024-10 Bender 2: Bend Harder
  • 2025-03 Shark Turtle, originally created 2023-06

Cancelled Projects

  • Aero Club—Flight school simulation inspired by Mode 7 and Pilotwings
  • Beatnik—Rhythm game with interactive music and beat-matched dynamic visuals
  • Cosmic Trash—Arcade action puzzle with realtime ray-traced graphics
  • Daily Driver—60fps arcade racing with pre-rendered graphics (reborn as Dream Ride)
  • Koatari—Pachinko-style arcade game with pinball-inspired visuals, running up to 120fps
  • Mole Hole—A modern remake of Thoru Yamamoto’s escape-maze concept

Notes

  • Daily Driver won “Outstanding Technical Achievement (SDK)” at the 2022 Community Awards
  • Sparrow Solitaire won “Best Puzzle Game” at the 2023 Community Awards
  • YOYOZO won “Best Arcade Game” at the 2023 Community Awards
  • YOYOZO was named one of the “Best Games of 2023” by Ars Technica
  • Bender 2: Bend Harder was nominated for “Best Arcade Game” at the 2024 Community Awards


Thanks to JM, JS, MR, PP, SC for feedback on versions of this post.
Panic were sent a draft of this post ahead of publication.
